title: Methods for making and using self-consolidating grout
abstract: Compositions and a method for making self consolidating grouts containing fly ash, ground granulated blast slag, and lime are described. No polymeric admixtures are utilized, and the 28 day cure strength of the cured gouts exceeds the minimum ASTM standards. The uncured grout exhibits slump flows between 24 and 30" without visible segregation of the components.
claims:
Claims C L A I M S 1. A self-consolidating grout mixture free of polymeric admixtures, comprising: a cementitious portion comprising a first portion of a cement, a second portion of a fly ash, a third portion of a ground granulated furnace blast slag, and a fourth portion of a hydrated lime; an aggregate portion comprising a fifth portion of dry aggregates and a sixth portion of adsorbed water; and a free water portion added to the cementitious portion and the aggregate portion to complete the self-consolidating grout mixture. 2. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein the aggregate portion comprises pea gravel and masonry sand. 3. The grout mixture as recited in claim 2, wherein the sixth portion of adsorbed water is primarily contained within the masonry sand. 4. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein the second portion of fly ash comprises Class F fly ash. 5. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein the second portion of fly ash has a weight between 3 and 4 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 6. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein the first portion of cement has a weight between 6 and 7 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 7. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein the third portion of ground granulated furnace blast slag has a weight between 6 and 7 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 8. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein the fourth portion of hydrated lime has a weight between 0.5 and 0.6 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 9. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein a weight of the total water content is approximately equal to a weight of the cementitious portion of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 10. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein a strength of the self-consolidated grout mixture after 28 days cure time is greater than 13.8 mpa (2000 psi). 11. The grout mixture as recited in claim 1, wherein a total water content of the self consolidating grout mixture comprises the free water portion and the sixth portion of adsorbed water in the aggregate portion. 12. A method for making a self-consolidating grout mixture free of polymeric admixtures, comprising: dry mixing components within a cementitious portion of the self-consolidating grout mixture, the components comprising a first portion of a cement, a second portion of a fly ash, and a third portion of a ground granulated furnace blast slag; providing a fourth portion of hydrated lime; determining a water content within an aggregate portion, the aggregate portion to be added to the self-consolidating grout mixture; 

 determining a free water content by subtracting the water content within the aggregate portion from a combined weight of the first portion of cement, the second portion of fly ash, the third portion of ground granulated furnace blast slag, and the fourth portion of hydrated lime; blending the free water content with the fourth portion of hydrated lime to form a hydrated lime suspension; adding the hydrated lime suspension to the dry mixed components of the cementitious portion to form a cementitious paste; and mixing the cementitious paste with the aggregate portion to complete formulation of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 13. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the first portion of cement, the second portion of fly ash, and the third portion of ground granulated furnace blast slag are each screened through #100 size sieves prior to dry mixing the cementitious portion of the self- consolidating grout mixture. 14. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein dry mixing of the cementitious portion of the self-consolidating grout mixture is completed when a uniform color of mixed solids is achieved. 15. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the hydrated lime suspension is added in a plurality of allocations, addition of each of the plurality of allocations being followed by mixing of the cementitious paste. 16. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein each of the plurality of allocations comprising between 10 and 33 wt% of the hydrated lime suspension. 17. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the aggregate portion comprises pea gravel and masonry sand. 18. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the aggregate portion comprises pea gravel and masonry sand, the pea gravel comprising between 20 and 21 wt% of the self- consolidating grout mixture. 19. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the aggregate portion comprises pea gravel and masonry sand, the masonry sand comprises between 45 and 46 wt% on a dry basis. 20. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the aggregate portion comprises pea gravel and masonry sand, the pea gravel comprising between 20 and 21 wt% of the self- consolidating grout mixture, and the masonry sand comprises between 45 and 46 wt% on a dry basis. 21. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the first portion of cement comprises between 6 and 7 wt% of the self consolidating grout mixture. 22. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the second portion of fly ash comprises Class F fly ash. 23. The method recited in claim 12, wherein the second portion of fly ash comprises Class F fly ash weighing between 3 and 4 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 24. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the third portion of ground granulated furnace blast slag comprises between 6 and 7 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 25. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein the fourth portion of hydrated lime comprises between 0.5 and 0.6 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture. 26. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein a strength of the self-consolidated grout mixture after 28 days cure time is greater than 13.8 mpa (2000 psi). 27. The method as recited in claim 12, wherein a strength of the self-consolidated grout mixture after 7 days cure time is greater than 10.3 mpa (1500 psi). 28. A grout mixture free of polymeric admixtures, comprising: a cementitious portion comprising a first portion of a cement, a second portion of a fly ash, a third portion of a ground granulated furnace blast slag, and a fourth portion of a hydrated lime; an aggregate portion comprising a fifth portion of dry aggregates and a sixth portion of adsorbed water; and a free water portion added to the cementitious portion and the aggregate portion to complete the self-consolidating grout mixture, wherein the second portion of fly ash has a weight between 3 and 4 wt% of the self-consolidating grout mixture, wherein the third portion of ground granulated furnace blast slag has a weight between 6 and 7 wt% of the self- consolidating grout mixture.
